analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-268

POL-268.title: IMPLEMENT: Polaris init and repository adoption workflow
POL-268.body:
## Objective

Implement the `polaris init` and `polaris init --adopt` workflows defined by [POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow).

## Context

This parent issue defines the full repository initialization and adoption workflow. The work extends `src/cli/init.ts` with two distinct flows:

* `polaris init` for new or empty repository initialization
* `polaris init --adopt` for existing repository adoption across Phase A, Phase B, and Phase C

The full order of operations, schemas, and handling rules are defined in [POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow) and `smartdocs/raw/pol-257-init-adoption-workflow-analysis.md`.

## Goal

Deliver a safe, ordered implementation of repo initialization and adoption that supports config locking, scanning, planning, approval, migration, cognition generation, instruction handling, atlas generation, and final staging or commit.

## Scope

* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” implement both init flows and their orchestration
* `polaris init` â€” scaffold, SmartDocs structure, and atlas baseline for new or empty repos
* `polaris init --adopt` â€” existing repo adoption flow across:
  * Phase A: config lock and scan
  * Phase B: plan generation and approval
  * Phase C: migration, cognition generation, instruction refactor, atlas generation, and stage or commit finalization
* Child execution set:
  * <issue id="c0f3ada5-9835-48e3-e546-00a9b75cd162">[POL-258](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-258/impl-add-repo-state-detection-to-polaris-init)</issue> â€” repo state detection
  * [POL-259](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-259/impl-add-read-only-repo-scanner-reposcaninventory) â€” read-only repo scanner
  * [POL-260](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-260/impl-add-adoption-plan-generator-adoptionplan) â€” adoption plan generator
  * [POL-261](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-261/impl-add-user-approval-gate-to-polaris-init-adopt) â€” user approval gate
  * [POL-262](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-262/impl-add-minimal-provider-config-lock-for-polaris-init-adopt) â€” minimal provider config lock
  * [POL-263](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-263/impl-add-smartdocs-migration-step-to-polaris-init-adopt) â€” SmartDocs migration step
  * [POL-264](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-264/impl-add-folder-cognition-generation-to-polaris-init-adopt) â€” folder cognition generation
  * [POL-265](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-265/impl-add-agent-instruction-file-handler-to-polaris-init-adopt) â€” agent instruction file handler
  * [POL-266](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-266/impl-integrate-atlas-generation-into-polaris-init-adopt) â€” atlas generation integration
  * [POL-267](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-267/impl-add-adoption-stagecommit-finalization-to-polaris-init-adopt) â€” stage or commit finalization

## Acceptance Criteria

* `polaris init` supports new or empty repository initialization.
* `polaris init --adopt` supports the full ordered adoption flow across all phases.
* Child steps cover config lock, scan, planning, approval, migration, cognition generation, instruction handling, atlas generation, and finalization.
* The implementation follows the order and handling rules defined by [POL-257](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-257/analyze-define-polaris-init-and-repository-adoption-workflow).

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`
* Validate both `polaris init` and `polaris init --adopt` flows end to end

## Ordering

Execute in child order:

1. <issue id="c0f3ada5-9835-48e3-e546-00a9b75cd162">[POL-258](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-258/impl-add-repo-state-detection-to-polaris-init)</issue>
2. [POL-259](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-259/impl-add-read-only-repo-scanner-reposcaninventory) and [POL-262](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-262/impl-add-minimal-provider-config-lock-for-polaris-init-adopt)
3. [POL-260](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-260/impl-add-adoption-plan-generator-adoptionplan)
4. [POL-261](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-261/impl-add-user-approval-gate-to-polaris-init-adopt)
5. [POL-263](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-263/impl-add-smartdocs-migration-step-to-polaris-init-adopt) and [POL-265](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-265/impl-add-agent-instruction-file-handler-to-polaris-init-adopt)
6. [POL-264](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-264/impl-add-folder-cognition-generation-to-polaris-init-adopt)
7. [POL-266](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-266/impl-integrate-atlas-generation-into-polaris-init-adopt)
8. [POL-267](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-267/impl-add-adoption-stagecommit-finalization-to-polaris-init-adopt)

## Non-goals

* Do not deviate from the analyzed order of operations without an explicit follow-up issue.
* Do not collapse adoption phases into a single unstructured flow.
* Do not change unrelated CLI behavior outside initialization and adoption.

POL-260.title: IMPL: Add adoption plan generator (AdoptionPlan)
POL-260.body:
## Objective

Implement an adoption plan generator that converts a `RepoScanInventory` into structured JSON and human-readable Markdown outputs.

## Context

The adoption flow needs a durable plan before interactive approval or mutation steps begin. The plan must summarize impact, sequence the work, and support `--dry-run` without executing Phase C writes.

## Goal

Create `generateAdoptionPlan(inventory: RepoScanInventory): AdoptionPlan` and produce both `.polaris/adoption-plan.json` and `.polaris/adoption-plan.md` so later steps can review, approve, and execute the adoption safely.

## Scope

* `src/cli/adopt-plan.ts` â€” implement `generateAdoptionPlan(inventory: RepoScanInventory): AdoptionPlan`
* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” wire `generateAdoptionPlan()` into the `--adopt` path
* `AdoptionPlan` output must include:
  * `plan_id`
  * `generated_at`
  * `repo_state`
  * `approved`
  * `approved_at`
  * `dry_run`
  * `steps`
  * `impact_summary`
* Each `AdoptionStep` must include order, phase, category, action, optional source and destination paths, description, approval requirement, risk, and execution status.
* `.polaris/adoption-plan.md` must include an impact summary table and grouped step lists by phase.
* When `--dry-run` is passed to `polaris init --adopt`, set `dry_run: true` and skip Phase C writes after reporting what would happen.

## Acceptance Criteria

* The generator produces valid JSON and Markdown plan outputs.
* Markdown output includes the impact summary table and grouped phase steps.
* `--dry-run` sets `dry_run: true` and skips Phase C writes.
* The plan remains usable by later approval and resume flows.

## Validation

* `npm test`
* `npm run build`

## Ordering

Depends on repo scanner output from [POL-259](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-259/impl-add-read-only-repo-scanner-reposcaninventory).

## Non-goals

* Do not execute adoption mutations in this issue.
* Do not add approval prompting in this issue.
* Do not bypass `--dry-run` safeguards.

POL-257.title: ANALYZE: Define Polaris init and repository adoption workflow

POL-263.title: IMPL: Add SmartDocs migration step to polaris init --adopt
POL-263.body:
## Objective

Implement the SmartDocs migration step in `polaris init --adopt` so discovered SmartDocs candidates are moved into `smartdocs/raw/` with provenance recorded.

## Context

This is Phase C, Step C2 of the adoption workflow. The migration must preserve history when possible, avoid moving excluded files, and remain resumable so repeated runs do not re-move already completed steps.

## Goal

Create `migrateSmartDocs(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>` and execute pending SmartDocs migration steps safely, with provenance tracking and resumable behavior.

## Scope

* `src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts` â€” implement `migrateSmartDocs(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>`
* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” wire the step into Phase C after `.polaris/` scaffold creation
* For each pending adoption step with `category: "smartdocs-migrate"`:
  1. Attempt `git mv <source_path> smartdocs/raw/<basename>`
  2. Fall back to `fs.rename()` if `git mv` fails and log the fallback
  3. Mark the step as completed in `.polaris/adoption-plan.json`
* Write provenance entries to `.polaris/adoption-provenance.json`
* Never move:
  * root `README.md`
  * `CHANGELOG.md`, `LICENSE`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`
  * files in `test/`, `fixtures/`, `__mocks__/`
  * files already under `smartdocs/`
  * generated documentation output
* On re-run, skip steps already marked completed and do not re-move files that were already moved

## Acceptance Criteria

* Pending SmartDocs migration steps move candidate files into `smartdocs/raw/`.
* Provenance records are written for migrated files.
* Excluded files are not moved.
* Re-runs skip completed migration steps without re-moving files.

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`

## Ordering

Depends on approval gating from [POL-261](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-261/impl-add-user-approval-gate-to-polaris-init-adopt) because migration runs only after approval.

## Non-goals

* Do not move excluded repository files even if they are detected as candidates.
* Do not silently lose history or provenance during migration.
* Do not re-run completed migration steps on resume.

POL-265.title: IMPL: Add agent instruction file handler to polaris init --adopt
POL-265.body:
## Objective

Handle existing agent instruction files during `polaris init --adopt` without silently deleting or truncating their content.

## Context

This is Phase C, Step C4 of the adoption workflow. Existing instruction files such as `CLAUDE.md`, `AGENTS.md`, Copilot instructions, Cursor rules, Aider config, and Gemini instruction files may already contain repository-specific behavior. Polaris needs preserve, migrate, or thin-adapter handling based on the current doctrine state and file contents.

## Goal

Create `handleInstructionFiles(plan: AdoptionPlan, inventory: RepoScanInventory): Promise<void>` so detected instruction files are classified and handled safely, with migrated content preserved and provenance recorded.

## Scope

* `src/cli/adopt-instructions.ts` â€” implement `handleInstructionFiles(plan: AdoptionPlan, inventory: RepoScanInventory): Promise<void>`
* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” wire the step into Phase C after SmartDocs migration and before atlas generation
* Detect instruction files including:
  * `CLAUDE.md`
  * `AGENTS.md`
  * `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
  * `.cursorrules` and `.cursor/rules/**/*.md`
  * `.aider.conf.yml` and `AIDER.md`
  * `**/GEMINI.md`
* Apply decision rules:
  * preserve if the file already contains Polaris delegation markers or no Polaris doctrine exists yet
  * thin-adapter if Polaris doctrine exists and the file is short and generic
  * migrate if Polaris doctrine exists and the file contains repository-specific content
* For migrate:
  1. Copy the original content to `smartdocs/raw/migrated-instructions/<filename>`
  2. Replace the original content with the thin adapter
  3. Record the change in adoption provenance
* Never delete or truncate an instruction file without first preserving its original content

## Acceptance Criteria

* Supported instruction files are detected and classified.
* Preserve, migrate, and thin-adapter behavior follows the documented rules.
* Migrated content is copied before replacement.
* Failures to preserve original content abort the step instead of deleting content.

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`

## Ordering

Depends on approval gating from [POL-261](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-261/impl-add-user-approval-gate-to-polaris-init-adopt) and runs before atlas generation.

## Non-goals

* Do not delete instruction files.
* Do not silently discard repository-specific instruction content.
* Do not expand this issue into atlas generation or unrelated adoption steps.

POL-266.title: IMPL: Integrate atlas generation into polaris init --adopt
POL-266.body:
## Objective

Run `polaris map index` as the final pre-stage atlas step of repository adoption and record the baseline coverage.

## Context

This is Phase C, Step C5 of the adoption workflow. After migration, cognition generation, and instruction handling, Polaris needs an atlas baseline that reflects the adopted repo state without overwriting the draft cognition files created earlier.

## Goal

Add `runAdoptionAtlas(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>` and execute atlas generation with adoption-safe options, then record baseline coverage for future validation comparisons.

## Scope

* `src/cli/adopt-atlas.ts` or `src/cli/init.ts` â€” add `runAdoptionAtlas(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>`
* `src/map/index.ts` â€” add `seedCognition` and `skipThreshold` options if needed
* Invoke `runMapIndex()` with:
  * `seedCognition: false`
  * no auto-write confidence threshold enforcement
* After indexing, read `.polaris/map/index.json` and write `adoption_baseline_coverage_pct`
* Verify at least one file from `smartdocs/raw/` appears in the atlas
* Log a warning, not an error, if `needs-review.json` exceeds 20% of total files

## Acceptance Criteria

* Adoption atlas generation runs with `seedCognition: false`.
* `.polaris/map/index.json` records `adoption_baseline_coverage_pct`.
* At least one `smartdocs/raw/` file is discoverable in the atlas.
* High adoption debt produces a warning rather than failing the step.

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`

## Ordering

Depends on cognition generation from [POL-264](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-264/impl-add-folder-cognition-generation-to-polaris-init-adopt) and instruction handling from [POL-265](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-265/impl-add-agent-instruction-file-handler-to-polaris-init-adopt).

## Non-goals

* Do not overwrite draft cognition files generated during adoption.
* Do not fail the run solely because adoption debt is high.
* Do not stage or commit changes in this issue.

POL-262.title: IMPL: Add minimal provider config lock for polaris init --adopt
POL-262.body:
## Objective

Write `polaris.config.json` with dispatch-safe defaults as the first write in `polaris init --adopt`, before the repository scan runs.

## Context

This is Phase A, Step A1 of the adoption flow and the only permitted write in Phase A. The adoption session needs a locked configuration surface before scanning so dispatch stays safe even when an existing repository already contains partial Polaris config.

This step must integrate with [POL-256](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-256/analyze-define-provider-role-config-fallback-and-dispatch-governance) without re-defining provider roles here.

## Goal

Add an adoption config lock that merges with existing config but forces the required locked keys to win for the adoption session, while remaining idempotent on resume.

## Scope

* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” add `writeAdoptionConfigLock()` before `scanRepo()`
* `src/config/loader.ts` â€” verify deep-merge behavior for locked keys
* Required locked config values:

```json
{
  "execution": {
    "rotation": [],
    "allowCrossAgentFallback": false,
    "adapter": "terminal-cli"
  },
  "orchestration": {
    "mode": "supervised"
  }
}
```

* Merge with an existing `polaris.config.json` when present, but the locked keys always win for the adoption session
* If the locked values are already present, skip the write and log `Provider config already locked â€” skipping.`
* Coordinate with [POL-256](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-256/analyze-define-provider-role-config-fallback-and-dispatch-governance) for the provider role hook point, but do not define provider roles here

## Acceptance Criteria

* `polaris.config.json` is written before any scan or plan generation.
* Locked keys cannot be overridden by a pre-existing partial config during the adoption session.
* The behavior is idempotent on resume.

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`

## Ordering

Depends on repo state detection from <issue id="c0f3ada5-9835-48e3-e546-00a9b75cd162">[POL-258](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-258/impl-add-repo-state-detection-to-polaris-init)</issue> and must run before the repo scan step.

## Non-goals

* Do not define provider role policy in this issue.
* Do not expand this step into scanner or plan-generation behavior.
* Do not allow pre-existing config to override the locked adoption-session safety keys.

POL-264.title: IMPL: Add folder cognition generation to polaris init --adopt
POL-264.body:
## Objective

Generate draft folder cognition files for stable canonical folders in an adopted repository.

## Context

This is Phase C, Step C3 of the adoption workflow. After SmartDocs migration, Polaris needs route-local draft cognition surfaces for stable folders, but these drafts must not be treated as canonical until explicitly promoted.

This step must align with [POL-233](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-233/analyze-normalize-smartdocs-routing-folder-cognition-yaml-links-and) and [POL-241](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-241/analyze-define-folder-local-work-note-staging-and-librarian).

## Goal

Create `generateFolderCognition(plan: AdoptionPlan, inventory: RepoScanInventory): Promise<void>` so eligible folders receive draft `POLARIS.md` and `SUMMARY.md` templates without polluting SmartDocs or runtime-generated areas.

## Scope

* `src/cli/adopt-cognition.ts` â€” implement `generateFolderCognition(plan: AdoptionPlan, inventory: RepoScanInventory): Promise<void>`
* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” wire the step into Phase C after SmartDocs migration
* Generate cognition files for folders in `inventory.likely_canonical_folders` that:
  * are not in generated, cache, or fixture roots
  * do not include `node_modules`, `dist`, `.git`, `.polaris/runs`, or `.polaris/bootstrap`
  * contain at least three source files
  * do not already contain `POLARIS.md`
* Generated files must use the draft template format and include the `<!-- polaris:draft -->` marker
* Do not create cognition files anywhere under `smartdocs/`
* Ensure later atlas indexing runs with `--seed-cognition=false` semantics so these generated drafts are not overwritten

## Acceptance Criteria

* Eligible canonical folders receive draft cognition files.
* Generated files include the `<!-- polaris:draft -->` marker.
* No cognition files are created in excluded or SmartDocs paths.
* Later indexing does not overwrite the generated draft cognition files.

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`

## Ordering

Depends on SmartDocs migration from [POL-263](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-263/impl-add-smartdocs-migration-step-to-polaris-init-adopt) because moved docs change the folder structure before cognition generation.

## Non-goals

* Do not treat generated draft cognition as canonical content.
* Do not generate cognition inside the `smartdocs/` subtree.
* Do not overwrite existing `POLARIS.md` files.

POL-261.title: IMPL: Add user approval gate to polaris init --adopt
POL-261.body:
## Objective

Add an explicit approval gate to `polaris init --adopt` so adoption cannot begin mutating repository content without user confirmation.

## Context

After the adoption plan is generated, the flow must pause, display the plan, and require approval before Phase C mutation begins. The flow also needs a `--yes` bypass and a resume behavior that respects already-approved plans.

## Goal

Implement `promptApproval(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<boolean>` so adoption only proceeds after approval is persisted, while also supporting `--yes` and `--resume` behavior.

## Scope

* `src/cli/adopt-approve.ts` or `src/cli/adopt-plan.ts` â€” add `promptApproval(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<boolean>`
* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” insert the approval gate between plan generation and Phase C mutation
* Required behavior:
  * Print `.polaris/adoption-plan.md` to stdout
  * Prompt `Proceed with adoption? [y/N]`
  * Treat empty input as no
  * On `y` or `Y`, set `plan.approved = true`, set `plan.approved_at`, persist the updated `.polaris/adoption-plan.json`, and continue
  * On any other input, print the abort message and return `false`
* `--yes` must skip the prompt, print a notice, and log the bypass in telemetry
* `polaris init --resume` must skip the prompt when `.polaris/adoption-plan.json` already has `approved: true`

## Acceptance Criteria

* Default answer is no and adoption does not proceed on empty input.
* `--yes` skips the prompt and is logged.
* Approved state is persisted before Phase C begins.
* Resume skips the prompt only when the plan is already approved.

## Validation

* `npm test`
* `npm run build`

## Ordering

Depends on adoption plan generation from [POL-260](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-260/impl-add-adoption-plan-generator-adoptionplan).

## Non-goals

* Do not begin Phase C mutations before persisting approval.
* Do not weaken the default-safe `y/N` behavior.
* Do not bundle unrelated adoption mutation steps into this issue.

POL-259.title: IMPL: Add read-only repo scanner (RepoScanInventory)
POL-259.body:
## Objective

Implement a read-only repository scanner that produces a `RepoScanInventory` describing the repo for adoption planning.

## Context

The adoption flow needs a structured inventory before any move or mutation work begins. The scanner must inspect the repo without changing files and produce a stable JSON output that later planning steps can consume.

## Goal

Create `scanRepo(): Promise<RepoScanInventory>` and write the resulting inventory to `.polaris/adoption-inventory.json` so the adoption planner can generate a sequenced execution plan.

## Scope

* `src/cli/adopt-scan.ts` â€” implement `scanRepo(): Promise<RepoScanInventory>`
* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” wire `scanRepo()` into the `--adopt` path
* `RepoScanInventory` output must include:
  * `scan_date`
  * `repo_state`
  * `package_manager`
  * `source_roots`
  * `docs_roots`
  * `test_commands`
  * `build_commands`
  * `package_scripts`
  * `generated_roots`
  * `cache_roots`
  * `fixture_roots`
  * `agent_instruction_files`
  * `existing_smartdocs_dirs`
  * `architecture_notes`
  * `likely_canonical_folders`
  * `smartdocs_candidates`
  * `ignore_candidates`
* Detection rules must cover package manager lockfiles, common agent instruction files, generated roots, SmartDocs candidates, and likely canonical folders.
* Write the result to `.polaris/adoption-inventory.json` and never overwrite an existing inventory without `--rescan`.

## Acceptance Criteria

* The scanner returns a `RepoScanInventory` matching the defined schema.
* The `--adopt` path writes `.polaris/adoption-inventory.json`.
* Existing inventory is preserved unless `--rescan` is explicitly provided.
* The scan is read-only aside from writing the inventory output.

## Validation

* `npm test`
* `npm run build`

## Ordering

Depends on repo state detection from [POL-258](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-258/impl-add-repo-state-detection-to-polaris-init). Run the scanner only for the `existing` state.

## Non-goals

* Do not move or modify repository content during the scan.
* Do not generate the adoption plan in this issue.
* Do not overwrite an existing inventory without explicit `--rescan`.

POL-258.title: IMPL: Add repo state detection to polaris init
POL-258.body:
## Objective

Add read-only repository state detection to `polaris init` so the command can determine the current repo initialization state before taking action.

## Context

Repository detection must happen before any adoption flow or mutation. Detection is strictly read-only and should classify the repo into one of five states: `empty`, `new`, `partial`, `existing`, or `polaris-enabled`.

The intended detection order is `polaris-enabled` â†’ `partial` â†’ `existing` â†’ `new` â†’ `empty`.

## Goal

Implement and export a typed `detectRepoState()` function and use it at the start of `runInit()` so adoption logic can branch correctly without writing files during detection.

## Scope

* `src/cli/init.ts` â€” add `detectRepoState()`, export a typed `RepoState` union, and call detection at the top of `runInit()`
* `src/cli/init-detect.ts` â€” optional extraction if detection should live in a separate module
* Detection criteria:
  * `empty` â€” directory is empty or contains only `.git/`
  * `new` â€” repository has `package.json` or basic source structure but no existing docs, agent files, or Polaris config
  * `partial` â€” repository has `polaris.config.json` but no `.polaris/map/file-routes.json`
  * `existing` â€” repository has source roots, docs, or agent instruction files but no `polaris.config.json`
  * `polaris-enabled` â€” repository has both `polaris.config.json` and `.polaris/map/file-routes.json`
* If the state is `existing` and `--adopt` is not present, print `This repo has existing content. Run polaris init --adopt to begin adoption.` and exit cleanly.

## Acceptance Criteria

* `detectRepoState()` is exported and returns a typed `RepoState` union.
* All five states are covered by unit tests.
* No file writes occur during detection.
* Existing `runInit()` behavior is unchanged for `empty` and `new` states.

## Validation

* `npm test`
* `npm run build`

## Ordering

Foundation step for the adoption workflow. Nothing depends on it being preceded by another child.

## Non-goals

* Do not write, move, or modify repository files during detection.
* Do not change adoption behavior beyond routing based on detected state.
* Do not expand this issue into scanner or plan-generation work.

POL-267.title: IMPL: Add adoption stage/commit finalization to polaris init --adopt
POL-267.body:
## Objective

Finalize repository adoption by staging the coherent adoption change set, appending runtime artifact exclusions to `.gitignore`, and optionally committing the result.

## Context

This is Phase C, Step C6 of the adoption workflow. After all adoption steps complete, Polaris must mark the plan complete, stage only the intended adoption outputs, exclude runtime artifacts from version control, and either leave the changes staged or commit them when `--commit` is provided.

## Goal

Add `finalizeAdoption(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>` so the adoption flow can end in a resumable, reviewable, and commit-safe state.

## Scope

* `src/cli/adopt-finalize.ts` or `src/cli/init.ts` â€” add `finalizeAdoption(plan: AdoptionPlan): Promise<void>`
* Append runtime artifact exclusions to `.gitignore` if missing:

```text
# Polaris runtime artifacts â€” do not commit
.polaris/runs/
.polaris/bootstrap/
.polaris/clusters/
.polaris/session-type
```

* Stage all adoption outputs including:
  * `.polaris/adoption-plan.json`
  * `.polaris/adoption-inventory.json`
  * `.polaris/adoption-provenance.json`
  * `polaris.config.json`
  * `.polaris/map/`
  * new `POLARIS.md` and `SUMMARY.md` files
  * moved or modified files from SmartDocs migration and instruction handling
  * `.gitignore`
* Do not stage `.polaris/runs/`, `.polaris/bootstrap/`, `.polaris/clusters/`, or `.polaris/session-type`
* If `--commit` is passed, create the adoption commit with the specified message format
* Otherwise, leave changes staged and print the review guidance message
* Mark all adoption steps as completed in `.polaris/adoption-plan.json` before staging
* If all steps are already complete on re-run, print `Adoption already complete.` and exit
* Print the adoption summary with moved file counts, cognition count, instruction count, baseline coverage, and excluded runtime artifact paths

## Acceptance Criteria

* Runtime artifact exclusions are present in `.gitignore`.
* Adoption outputs are staged while excluded runtime artifacts remain unstaged.
* `--commit` creates the adoption commit, and the non-commit path leaves changes staged.
* Re-runs exit cleanly once all adoption steps are complete.

## Validation

* `npm run build`
* `npm test`

## Ordering

Depends on atlas generation from [POL-266](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-266/impl-integrate-atlas-generation-into-polaris-init-adopt).

## Non-goals

* Do not stage or commit runtime artifact directories.
* Do not bypass marking plan steps complete before staging.
* Do not collapse this issue into earlier adoption steps.
